puzzle.title: The travelers of the verdant field
puzzle.intro: In an ancient time, two individuals tended neighboring lands. Their paths were intertwined, yet their hearts led them toward vastly different destinations. One walked the straight path, while the other strayed into the darkness of denial. Can you unravel the mystery of these two keepers of the harvest?
clues: I possessed abundant crops, luxuriant harvests that dazzled the eyes of onlookers.
My neighbor possessed even greater bounty than I did, yet I coveted their portion more than my own.
I suggested we destroy their crops entirely, to consolidate everything under my control.
I brought ruin upon my own harvest as well, destroying everything in my foolish scheming.
I stood helpless as my crops withered and my wealth turned to dust in a single night.
I had no share in the Hereafter, and my final abode became a pit of blazing fire.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The man who denied resurrection and judgment
The ruler who oppressed the righteous farmer
The wealthy landowner who lacked gratitude
The farmer who coveted their neighbor's land
The treacherous partner who plotted destruction
identity.answer: The farmer who coveted their neighbor's land

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: The test of receiving divine revelation
The challenge of wealth and patience
The temptation to destroy a righteous person's livelihood
The command to divide inheritance fairly
The struggle against a tyrant's oppression
trial.answer: The temptation to destroy a righteous person's livelihood

location.label: Key Element
options: The verdant gardens of paradise
Two neighboring fields of crops
The mountains that witnessed destruction
The sacred sanctuary of Makkah
The riverbanks of a blessed land

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Eternal paradise as a reward for righteousness
Complete destruction of both harvests in this life
Exile and wandering until death
Temporary prosperity followed by eternal fire
A miraculous restoration and doubled bounty
outcome.answer: Complete destruction of both harvests in this life

puzzle.verse: Then they approached one another, blaming each other. (68:30)
